resources.name: TV5MONDE — Apprendre le français
resources.why: The largest free official French-learning platform, from the Francophone public broadcaster. Thousands of exercises by CEFR level (A1-B2) built on real video and audio, with listening, reading, grammar and vocabulary. Your main input source: the app schedules it level by level so there's never a question of what to do next.

resources.name: RFI Savoirs — Le français facile
resources.why: Radio France Internationale's free service for learners: the daily news read slowly (journal en français facile) plus graded listening exercises. The best official source for DELF-style listening comprehension (compréhension de l'oral) from A2 up.

resources.name: France Éducation international — DELF/DALF
resources.why: The state body that designs and awards the DELF. Publishes the official sample papers (exemples de sujets) the app's mock exams mirror, explains the exam format and pass rule, and is where you book the real B1/B2 exam. The finish line.

resources.name: Référentiels — Niveau A1 à B2 pour le français (Didier)
resources.why: The official CEFR reference-level descriptions for French (Council of Europe / Didier): the per-level inventory of grammar, notions and functions the app's curriculum is anchored to. The 'no shortcuts' correctness layer.

resources.name: Conversation partner
resources.why: Speaking is one of the four DELF sections. Nothing replaces real conversation: a tutor, a language exchange, or a French-speaking friend. The app's dialogues give you scripts to practise; a real partner turns them into fluency.

resources.name: Words tab (built-in daily flashcards)
resources.why: The app's own spaced-repetition deck: the ~2,900 highest-leverage French words through B2, introduced in frequency order with example sentences and the French voice. Your daily vocabulary engine, scheduled every session.
